macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Indian Stock Market Shows Weakness as Nifty Breaks Key Technical Levels
items.summary: The Nifty index has broken important technical support levels amid geopolitical tensions, rising crude oil prices, FII selling, and currency weakness. Bank Nifty declined nearly 7% last week, with key support levels identified at 13,300-13,400.

items.title: Iran Escalates Gulf Tensions, Threatens to Block Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: Senior Iranian official Mohsen Rezaee declared the Strait of Hormuz 'will not be opened' to American ships, warning of potential massive spikes in global oil prices. He predicted US strategic reserve releases would only provide short-term price stabilization.

items.title: Trump Threatens Additional Strikes on Iran's Kharg Island Oil Export Hub
items.summary: US President Donald Trump stated the military could strike Iran's Kharg Island again 'a few more times… just for fun,' claiming previous strikes had 'totally demolished' most of the island. The remarks escalate tensions over control of the critical Strait of Hormuz shipping route.

items.title: US Senate Questions Military Leadership on Iran Conflict Setbacks
items.summary: Senator Mark Kelly pressed senior US military commanders during a Senate Armed Services Committee hearing over reported battlefield challenges and troop safety concerns in the escalating Iran conflict. Lawmakers raised concerns about casualties, resource strain, and risks of wider regional war.

items.title: Gold Faces Pressure as Interest Rate Cut Expectations Fade
items.summary: Gold prices remain under pressure as fading expectations for interest rate cuts outweigh traditional safe-haven demand. Technical analysis suggests potential selling opportunities around 5,096-5,150 levels.
items.source: KG Trading - Stock Market Elliott Wave

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Trades Sideways Near $72K as Market Shows Cautious Recovery
items.summary: Bitcoin is consolidating around $71,000-$72,000 on March 15, 2026, down 43% from its $126,000 all-time high, amid a broader market recovery driven by institutional ETF inflows and macroeconomic sensitivity. Upcoming US Federal Reserve rate decision on March 18 and 91 crypto ETF decisions by March 27 could trigger a breakout.
items.source: Binance Square, Bitcoin.com News, Sergey Tereshkin

items.title: US CLARITY Act Faces Delays, Reducing 2026 Passage Odds
items.summary: The US CLARITY Act, aimed at providing regulatory clarity for crypto including stablecoins and DeFi, risks low passage chances in 2026 if it misses committee by end of April, amid debates over stablecoin rewards impacting banks. Galaxy Digital's Alex Thorn highlights needs for Senate floor by early May.

items.title: Crypto Market Shifts to Selective Altcoin Positioning Amid Bitcoin Leadership
items.summary: The market is moving from broad rallies to targeted investments in liquid assets like Bitcoin and Ethereum, with altcoins like Cardano gaining mid-term interest, influenced by stock markets, monetary policy, and geopolitics. Institutional demand via ETFs bolsters the sector's integration into global finance.

items.title: Four AI Models Predict Bitcoin to Hit $100K Again in 2026
items.summary: ChatGPT, Grok, DeepSeek, and Gemini forecast Bitcoin reclaiming $100K in 2026, while only Claude disagrees, based on analysis of market trends and predictions.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: US Mayors Resist Data Center Expansion Amid Growing AI Backlash
items.summary: US mayors are pushing back against the boom in data centers driven by AI demands, reflecting increasing public and local opposition to the infrastructure needs of AI growth.

items.title: Tech Hiring Shifts in 2026: AI Drives Demand for Specialists Amid Fewer Roles
items.summary: India's tech job market sees rising demand for AI, machine learning, and data specialists while traditional software roles slow due to automation; companies prioritize hybrid skills in problem-solving and analytics.

items.title: Must-Have AI Skills for 2026 Hiring: Generative AI, ML, and Cloud Expertise
items.summary: Hiring trends emphasize generative AI, prompt engineering, data science, machine learning model development, and AI-cloud integration using tools like TensorFlow, PyTorch, AWS, and Azure.

items.title: Rise of Agentic AI: 5 Key Breakthroughs Reshaping Business in March 2026
items.summary: Agentic AI emerges as a major trend with five critical breakthroughs transforming business operations and the future of work this month.

items.title: Expanding AI Literacy Framework Addresses 2026 Challenges in Evidence and Narratives
items.summary: New AI literacy taxonomy covers understanding ML fundamentals, critical evaluation of AI outputs, evidence from studies like Anthropic's 2026 labor market analysis, and countering AI-washing in economic narratives.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Slay the Spire 2 Achieves Major Success on Launch
items.summary: Slay the Spire 2 has become a major success, praised as a massive upgrade to the original while staying true to its formula. The game surprised players with its similarity to the first entry, embodying 'if it ain’t broke, don’t fix it'.

items.title: Xenonauts 2 Full Version Set for April Release After 10 Years
items.summary: Xenonauts 2, a spiritual successor to classic XCOM, will exit Early Access with its full version in April following a decade of development and a successful 2018 Kickstarter. The game entered Early Access in 2023.

items.title: Valve Updates Steam Machine Release to 'This Year' Amid Delays
items.summary: Valve has changed its Steam Machine release promise from early 2026 to simply 'this year,' following earlier shifts to first half of 2026, amid AI-fueled memory shortages. Initial posts hinted at potential 2027 delays before revision.

items.title: Nvidia Announces DLSS 4.5 Launch on March 31 at GDC
items.summary: Nvidia revealed at GDC that DLSS 4.5 features, including improved second-gen transformer model for Super Resolution and frame generation supporting up to 6x frames, will go live on March 31. Gaming announcements were described as lackluster overall.

items.title: Microsoft Teases Project Helix Xbox Mode for PCs at GDC
items.summary: Microsoft announced Project Helix, an 'Xbox Mode' for PCs, at GDC, countering rumors of sunsetting gaming efforts and teasing next-gen Xbox console developments.

items.title: Intel Announces Arrow Lake Refresh Desktop CPUs
items.summary: Intel revealed 'Arrow Lake Refresh' Core Ultra 270K/250K Plus desktop CPUs, part of broader PC hardware updates discussed in gaming context at GDC.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.
